question: [File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/persistence/transaction.go -> Scope: Medium: A bug in the respective layer 0/1/2 network code that results in unintended smart contract behavior with no concrete funds at direct risk'] [Function: constructTransaction()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a transaction with type int16 that maps to an unknown transaction type string via types.GetTransactionType(), causing constructTransaction() to emit operations with type 'UNKNOWN', and smart contract consumers that whitelist operation types to reject or mishandle the transaction?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/persistence/transaction.go -> Scope: Medium: A bug in the respective layer 0/1/2 network code that results in unintended smart contract behavior with no concrete funds at direct risk'] [Function: constructTransaction()] Can an unprivileged external user cause constructTransaction() to set result.EntityId to the EntityId from the last successful sub-transaction (overwriting earlier values in the loop), causing smart contract observers to see the wrong entity ID associated with a multi-record transaction?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/persistence/transaction.go -> Scope: Medium: A bug in the respective layer 0/1/2 network code that results in unintended smart contract behavior with no concrete funds at direct risk'] [Function: FindBetween()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that FindBetween() uses the last transaction's ConsensusTimestamp+1 as the new start in the batch loop, and if multiple transactions share the same maximum timestamp in a batch of 2000, cause those transactions to be re-fetched in the next batch iteration and duplicated in the sameHashMap?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/persistence/transaction.go -> Scope: Medium: A bug in the respective layer 0/1/2 network code that results in unintended smart contract behavior with no concrete funds at direct risk'] [Function: categorizeHbarTransfers()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a transaction with an itemized transfer to the staking reward entity ID and non-empty stakingRewardPayouts, causing that itemized transfer to appear in both adjustedItemizedTransfers and be factored into the staking reward debit calculation via aggregateHbarTransfers(), leading to double-counting in the smart contract operation view?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/persistence/transaction.go -> Scope: Medium: A bug in the respective layer 0/1/2 network code that results in unintended smart contract behavior with no concrete funds at direct risk'] [Function: hbarTransfer.getAmount()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a transaction with an hbarTransfer.Amount of math.MinInt64, causing getAmount() to return an HbarAmount with Value=MinInt64, and any downstream negation of this value to overflow, producing incorrect balance representations in smart contract state?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/persistence/transaction.go -> Scope: Medium: A bug in the respective layer 0/1/2 network code that results in unintended smart contract behavior with no concrete funds at direct risk'] [Function: FindByHashInBlock()] Can an unprivileged external user pass a valid hex hash that exists in the database but outside the provided consensusStart/consensusEnd range, causing FindByHashInBlock() to return ErrTransactionNotFound, and smart contract logic that retries with different block ranges to be confused about transaction finality?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/persistence/transaction.go -> Scope: Medium: A bug in the respective layer 0/1/2 network code that results in unintended smart contract behavior with no concrete funds at direct risk'] [Function: constructTransaction()] Can an unprivileged external user cause constructTransaction() to process sameHashTransactions where the first element has a nil EntityId, causing result.EntityId to remain nil even if a later sub-transaction has a non-nil EntityId (since EntityId is only set when IsTransactionResultSuccessful), and smart contract observers to miss the created entity?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/persistence/transaction.go -> Scope: Medium: A bug in the respective layer 0/1/2 network code that results in unintended smart contract behavior with no concrete funds at direct risk'] [Function: categorizeHbarTransfers()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a transaction where the same entity ID appears in both the itemized transfer list and the staking reward payouts list, causing categorizeHbarTransfers() to include it in adjustedItemizedTransfers via the entity ID existence check and also in stakingRewardTransfers, resulting in the same account appearing in multiple operation categories in the Rosetta response?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/persistence/transaction.go -> Scope: Medium: A bug in the respective layer 0/1/2 network code that results in unintended smart contract behavior with no concrete funds at direct risk'] [Function: appendTransferOperations()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a transaction that generates a very large number of operations via appendTransferOperations(), causing the operation Index (cast from int64(len(operations))) to grow without bound, and smart contract consumers that validate index ranges to reject the transaction or behave unexpectedly?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/persistence/transaction.go -> Scope: Medium: A bug in the respective layer 0/1/2 network code that results in unintended smart contract behavior with no concrete funds at direct risk'] [Function: constructTransaction()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a transaction where the Memo field contains non-UTF8 bytes, causing constructTransaction() to set result.Memo to raw bytes that downstream smart contract consumers parsing memo as a string may misinterpret or crash on?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/persistence/transaction.go -> Scope: Medium: A bug in the respective layer 0/1/2 network code that results in unintended smart contract behavior with no concrete funds at direct risk'] [Function: FindBetween()] Can an unprivileged external user query FindBetween() with a range containing transactions whose hashes are all identical (hash collision scenario), causing the sameHashMap to accumulate all transactions under one key and constructTransaction() to merge all their operations into a single transaction, misrepresenting the block's transaction count to smart contract observers?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/persistence/transaction.go -> Scope: Medium: A bug in the respective layer 0/1/2 network code that results in unintended smart contract behavior with no concrete funds at direct risk'] [Function: categorizeHbarTransfers()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a transaction with an empty itemizedTransfer slice but non-empty stakingRewardPayouts, causing categorizeHbarTransfers() to compute a non-zero rewardPayoutTotal and append a debit entry for the staking reward entity to stakingRewardTransfers, which then gets passed to aggregateHbarTransfers() and affects fee calculation for smart contract observers?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/persistence/transaction.go -> Scope: Medium: A bug in the respective layer 0/1/2 network code that results in unintended smart contract behavior with no concrete funds at direct risk'] [Function: IsTransactionResultSuccessful()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a transaction with result code 22 (transactionResultSuccess) that actually represents a failed smart contract execution at the EVM level, causing IsTransactionResultSuccessful() to return true and Rosetta to report the transaction as successful, misleading smart contract state monitors?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/persistence/transaction.go -> Scope: Medium: A bug in the respective layer 0/1/2 network code that results in unintended smart contract behavior with no concrete funds at direct risk'] [Function: constructTransaction()] Can an unprivileged external user cause constructTransaction() to emit staking reward transfer operations with type OperationTypeCryptoTransfer and status 'SUCCESS' regardless of the parent transaction result, causing smart contract logic that checks operation type to misclassify staking reward debits as regular crypto transfers?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/persistence/transaction.go -> Scope: Medium: A bug in the respective layer 0/1/2 network code that results in unintended smart contract behavior with no concrete funds at direct risk'] [Function: getFeeHbarTransfers()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a transaction where the crypto transfer amount for an account is negative and the itemized transfer amount for the same account is also negative, causing getFeeHbarTransfers() to compute amount = negative - negative = potentially positive, misclassifying a debit as a credit in the fee operations seen by smart contract monitors?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/persistence/transaction.go -> Scope: Medium: A bug in the respective layer 0/1/2 network code that results in unintended smart contract behavior with no concrete funds at direct risk'] [Function: FindBetween()] Can an unprivileged external user trigger the batch loop in FindBetween() to advance start past end by having the last transaction in a full batch have ConsensusTimestamp equal to end, causing start to become end+1 which is > end, and the loop to terminate without fetching a potential second batch, silently dropping transactions at the boundary?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/persistence/transaction.go -> Scope: Medium: A bug in the respective layer 0/1/2 network code that results in unintended smart contract behavior with no concrete funds at direct risk'] [Function: constructTransaction()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a transaction where the Type field is a negative int16 value, causing types.GetTransactionType(int32(firstTransaction.Type)) to receive a negative int32 and return an unexpected type string, leading smart contract consumers to misclassify the transaction operation type?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/persistence/transaction.go -> Scope: Medium: A bug in the respective layer 0/1/2 network code that results in unintended smart contract behavior with no concrete funds at direct risk'] [Function: categorizeHbarTransfers()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a transaction with a staking reward payout of zero amount, causing categorizeHbarTransfers() to append a zero-amount entry to stakingRewardTransfers and compute rewardPayoutTotal=0, skipping the debit entry for the staking reward account, resulting in an unbalanced operation set visible to smart contract monitors?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/persistence/transaction.go -> Scope: Medium: A bug in the respective layer 0/1/2 network code that results in unintended smart contract behavior with no concrete funds at direct risk'] [Function: FindByHashInBlock()] Can an unprivileged external user pass a hash string consisting only of the hex prefix '0x' with no following bytes to FindByHashInBlock(), causing hex.DecodeString on an empty string to succeed and return an empty byte slice, which then matches any transaction with a zero-length hash in the database?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/persistence/transaction.go -> Scope: Medium: A bug in the respective layer 0/1/2 network code that results in unintended smart contract behavior with no concrete funds at direct risk'] [Function: constructTransaction()] Can an unprivileged external user cause constructTransaction() to process a sameHashTransactions slice where sub-transactions have different Type values, but only firstTransaction.Type is used for transactionType, causing all operations to be labeled with the first sub-transaction's type even if later sub-transactions are of a different type?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/persistence/transaction.go -> Scope: Medium: A bug in the respective layer 0/1/2 network code that results in unintended smart contract behavior with no concrete funds at direct risk'] [Function: appendHbarTransferOperations()] Can an unprivileged external user cause appendHbarTransferOperations() to emit fee operations with the hardcoded 'success' status string even for a transaction that was reverted at the smart contract level, causing Rosetta consumers to see fee operations as successful while the contract call itself failed, creating an inconsistent operation status view?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/persistence/transaction.go -> Scope: Medium: A bug in the respective layer 0/1/2 network code that results in unintended smart contract behavior with no concrete funds at direct risk'] [Function: categorizeHbarTransfers()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a transaction where the itemized transfer slice contains an entry with EntityId.EncodedId=0 (zero entity), causing categorizeHbarTransfers() to check entityIds[0] and potentially match the zero-value entity ID that appears in uninitialized EntityId structs, incorrectly including that transfer in adjustedItemizedTransfers?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/persistence/transaction.go -> Scope: Medium: A bug in the respective layer 0/1/2 network code that results in unintended smart contract behavior with no concrete funds at direct risk'] [Function: FindBetween()] Can an unprivileged external user query FindBetween() with a range that spans multiple batches, and if a transaction's hash appears in both the last record of one batch and the first record of the next batch (due to the start+1 advancement), cause that transaction to be processed twice and appear twice in the result, corrupting the operation set seen by smart contract monitors?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/persistence/transaction.go -> Scope: Medium: A bug in the respective layer 0/1/2 network code that results in unintended smart contract behavior with no concrete funds at direct risk'] [Function: constructTransaction()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a transaction where the Result field is a negative int16 value, causing types.GetTransactionResult(int32(transaction.Result)) to receive a negative int32 and return an unexpected result string, leading smart contract consumers to misclassify the transaction status?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/persistence/transaction.go -> Scope: Medium: A bug in the respective layer 0/1/2 network code that results in unintended smart contract behavior with no concrete funds at direct risk'] [Function: categorizeHbarTransfers()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a transaction where the stackingRewardEntityId (note: typo in parameter name) passed to categorizeHbarTransfers() is the zero EntityId (EncodedId=0), causing the staking reward exception check to match any itemized transfer with a zero entity ID and incorrectly include it in adjustedItemizedTransfers?
